name: Variance Analysis — 50Q
description: 50 variance attribution questions testing revenue, cost, margin, and EBITDA bridge analysis with driver-level attribution.

cases.name: Revenue Miss Q4
cases.input: Why did revenue miss budget in Q4 FY2025?
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue missed by $4.2M (-4.3%) due to delayed enterprise contract renewals (-$3.1M) and lower PS volume (-$1.1M).

cases.name: EBITDA vs Budget
cases.input: What caused EBITDA to fall short of budget?
cases.expectedOutput: EBITDA missed by $2.8M: revenue shortfall of $4.2M partially offset by underspend in headcount ($1.0M) and T&E ($0.4M).

cases.name: Gross Margin Miss
cases.input: Why did gross margin come in below plan?
cases.expectedOutput: Gross margin was 67.3% vs plan of 68.2%, an 80bps shortfall driven by higher hosting costs and CS team additions.

cases.name: Opex Variance
cases.input: Were operating expenses above or below budget?
cases.expectedOutput: Total OpEx was $2.1M below budget, primarily from $1.0M headcount underspend (open roles) and $0.4M lower T&E. Marketing also underspent by $0.7M.

cases.name: ARR Bookings Miss
cases.input: Did we hit our ARR bookings target this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: New logo bookings of $14M missed the $18M target. Expansion bookings of $8M were on plan.

cases.name: Enterprise Revenue Variance
cases.input: How did enterprise segment revenue perform vs budget?
cases.expectedOutput: Enterprise revenue was $4.2M below budget due to 3 delayed contract renewals expected in Q4 but now forecast for Q1.

cases.name: PS Revenue Variance
cases.input: What drove the professional services revenue shortfall?
cases.expectedOutput: PS revenue was $1.1M below budget due to 2 implementation projects pushed to Q1 and lower-than-expected SOW expansions.

cases.name: Headcount vs Plan
cases.input: How does actual headcount compare to plan?
cases.expectedOutput: Actual headcount is 12 below plan due to 8 open engineering roles and 4 sales roles not yet backfilled after Q3 departures.

cases.name: Marketing Underspend
cases.input: Why did marketing underspend vs budget?
cases.expectedOutput: Marketing was $0.7M below budget: delayed campaign launches ($0.4M) and reduced conference spend ($0.3M) following event cancellations.

cases.name: T&E Variance
cases.input: What is the variance in travel and entertainment expenses?
cases.expectedOutput: T&E was $0.4M favorable vs budget. Sales travel was constrained by Q4 hiring delays and shift to virtual QBRs.

cases.name: Revenue Driver Attribution
cases.input: Attribute the Q4 revenue variance to price, volume, and mix effects.
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue miss $4.2M: Volume effect -$3.8M (fewer enterprise deals closed), Price effect +$0.2M (5% YoY price increase on renewals), Mix effect -$0.6M (higher PS, lower subscription mix vs budget).

cases.name: COGS Variance Attribution
cases.input: What drove the COGS variance this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: COGS was $1.4M above budget: hosting $0.6M (volume inflation), CS headcount $0.5M (new hires), amortization $0.3M (capitalized software). These are structural increases unlikely to reverse.

cases.name: EBITDA Bridge Q3 to Q4
cases.input: Build the EBITDA bridge from Q3 to Q4 with driver-level detail.
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 EBITDA $18.2M → Q4 $15.72M (-$2.48M): Revenue -$4.2M, COGS headwinds -$1.4M, S&M underspend +$1.3M, G&A underspend +$0.5M, R&D underspend +$0.32M, Other +$1.0M = net -$2.48M.

cases.name: Gross Margin Bridge
cases.input: Build the gross margin bridge from Q3 to Q4.
cases.expectedOutput: Q3 GM 68.1% → Q4 67.3% (-80bps): Hosting cost inflation -30bps, CS additions -40bps, PS mix shift +20bps, Amortization -30bps. Net -80bps.

cases.name: Sales Efficiency Decline
cases.input: Why did sales efficiency decline this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: Magic Number fell from 1.1x to 0.9x: S&M spend grew 18% while Net New ARR declined 35% QoQ. Root cause: 3 large enterprise deals slipped; S&M investment was made in advance of expected deal flow.

cases.name: Churn Variance
cases.input: Was gross churn above or below plan this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: Gross churn was $4.0M, in-line with the $4.2M plan. No material deviations by segment. SMB churn was $0.2M favorable vs plan.

cases.name: New Logo vs Expansion Variance
cases.input: How did new logo and expansion perform vs plan?
cases.expectedOutput: New logo was $4M below plan ($14M vs $18M); expansion was on plan at $8M. New logo miss driven by 3 enterprise deals slipping to Q1. Expansion showing strong health.

cases.name: ARR Waterfall vs Plan
cases.input: Compare the actual ARR waterfall to plan.
cases.expectedOutput: Plan: +$28M net new ARR. Actual: +$18M (-$10M). New bookings miss -$4M, expansion on plan, churn on plan. Opening ARR on plan.

cases.name: R&D Efficiency Variance
cases.input: Did R&D spend come in above or below budget?
cases.expectedOutput: R&D was $0.32M below budget ($17.3M vs $17.6M plan) due to delayed contractor onboarding. No capitalization difference.

cases.name: NRR Variance
cases.input: Did net revenue retention hit target this quarter?
cases.expectedOutput: NRR was 112% vs 115% target. Enterprise NRR was 121% (on plan). Mid-Market 113% (on plan). SMB 97% vs 102% target (-5pp shortfall from higher SMB contraction).

cases.name: Full Quarter Variance Summary
cases.input: Produce a full P&L variance summary for Q4 FY2025 vs budget.
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue -$4.2M (-4.3%), Gross Profit -$5.6M (margin 67.3% vs 68.2%), S&M favorable $1.3M, R&D favorable $0.32M, G&A favorable $0.5M. EBITDA -$2.8M (-15.1%). Net variance: opex management partially absorbed the revenue miss.

cases.name: Materiality Assessment
cases.input: Which Q4 variances are material and require controller sign-off?
cases.expectedOutput: Material variances (>5% of budget or >$1M): Revenue -$4.2M (requires CFO commentary), EBITDA -$2.8M (requires controller sign-off). Non-material: individual opex line items all <$1M and <5% of budget. SOX threshold: $10M. No items exceed SOX threshold.

cases.name: Enterprise Deal Slippage Analysis
cases.input: Analyze the 3 enterprise deals that slipped from Q4 to Q1.
cases.expectedOutput: Deal 1: $1.8M ACV, procurement delay, signed Jan 8. Deal 2: $0.8M ACV, legal redline, signing pending. Deal 3: $0.5M ACV, decision-maker change, re-engaging. Total slipped ARR: $3.1M. Revenue impact Q4: -$1.55M (half-quarter recognition if signed by Dec 31).

cases.name: Margin Compression Forecast
cases.input: Will gross margin compression continue into FY2026?
cases.expectedOutput: Structural headwinds likely to persist: hosting +20bps, amortization +30bps. Potential offsets: pricing increase (+15bps), automation savings (+10bps). Net: additional 25bps compression in H1 FY2026, stabilizing by Q3 as capitalized software reaches steady-state amortization.

cases.name: One-Time vs Recurring Variance
cases.input: Separate the Q4 variance into one-time vs recurring components.
cases.expectedOutput: One-time items: restructuring $0.8M, legal $0.2M, lease termination $0.3M. Total one-time expenses $1.3M. Recurring variance: revenue miss $4.2M (2/3 cyclical, 1/3 structural), COGS +$1.4M (structural/recurring). Normalized EBITDA $17.4M vs reported $15.7M.

cases.name: Opex Rate Analysis
cases.input: Analyze whether our opex rates are improving or deteriorating.
cases.expectedOutput: As % of revenue: S&M 24% (plan 23%, +100bps — slight deterioration from lower rev). R&D 18.4% (plan 17.9%, +50bps — headcount ramp). G&A 9.1% (plan 8.6%, +50bps — fixed cost deleverage on lower rev). All rates deteriorated due to revenue miss creating fixed cost deleverage, not spending excess.

cases.name: Cohort Variance
cases.input: Which customer cohort is showing the largest negative variance vs plan?
cases.expectedOutput: FY2022 mid-market cohort is the largest negative variance: $1.2M ARR churn (expected $0.4M) from 3 contract non-renewals. Root cause: product-market fit issues with new feature set introduced in Q2. FY2024 cohorts are performing on plan or better.

cases.name: Geographic Revenue Variance
cases.input: How did revenue vary by geography vs budget?
cases.expectedOutput: North America -$2.8M (enterprise deal slippage). EMEA +$0.4M (beat on 2 new logos). APAC -$1.8M (later-than-expected market entry). Net miss: -$4.2M. EMEA is the strongest performing region.

cases.name: SaaS Metrics Variance Dashboard
cases.input: What SaaS metrics are most off-track vs plan?
cases.expectedOutput: Most off-track: New Logo ARR -22%, Sales Magic Number -18%, SMB NRR -5pp. On-track: Enterprise NRR, Gross Churn, Expansion ARR, Gross Margin (within 80bps). Pipeline coverage is lagging at 2.4x vs 3x target — leading indicator of potential Q1 miss.

cases.name: COGS Inflation Drivers
cases.input: What is driving COGS inflation and how should we address it?
cases.expectedOutput: COGS inflation sources: (1) Cloud infrastructure +$0.6M (volume growth + per-unit cost increases from provider). (2) CS headcount +$0.5M (proactive capacity adds for Q1 onboarding). (3) Amortization +$0.3M (new modules going live). Mitigation: cloud cost optimization ($0.3M opportunity), defer non-critical CS hire, amortization will plateau Q2 FY2026.

cases.name: Full-Year FY2025 Variance
cases.input: Summarize the full-year FY2025 variance vs budget.
cases.expectedOutput: FY2025: Revenue $374M vs $389M budget (-$15M, -3.9%). EBITDA $58M vs $72M budget (-$14M). Gross Margin 67.8% vs 68.5%. ARR $312M vs $328M. S&M underspend $4.2M offset revenue leverage. Multiple factors: macro headwinds in H2, 3 large deal slippages, hosting cost inflation.

cases.name: Revenue Recognition Timing
cases.input: Are there revenue recognition timing differences affecting the variance?
cases.expectedOutput: $1.2M of revenue recognized in Q4 was deferred from Q3 (delivery completed Q4). $0.8M of expected Q4 revenue deferred to Q1 (incomplete milestones). Net timing impact: +$0.4M, non-recurring.

cases.name: FX Impact
cases.input: What was the foreign exchange impact on revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: FX had a -$0.6M impact on reported revenue (EMEA billed in EUR, GBP; APAC in USD). Constant currency revenue miss would be -$3.6M vs reported -$4.2M.

cases.name: Vertical Variance
cases.input: Which industry vertical performed best vs budget?
cases.expectedOutput: FinServ +$0.8M above budget (strong regulatory tailwind driving urgency). Tech -$2.1M (budget freezes in Q4). Healthcare on plan. FinServ is emerging as the most resilient vertical.

cases.name: Customer Success Variance
cases.input: How did customer success metrics perform vs targets?
cases.expectedOutput: CSAT 4.2/5 (target 4.3, -0.1). Time-to-value 42 days (target 35, +7 days). NPS 42 (target 45, -3). CS team was 2 FTE below plan due to open roles, impacting service capacity.

cases.name: Bookings Quality
cases.input: Was the quality of bookings this quarter in line with historical patterns?
cases.expectedOutput: Average ACV $136K vs $128K LTM average (+6%). Contract length 24.3 months vs 22.8 months average (+7%). Multi-year contracts 34% of bookings (vs 28% plan). Bookings quality is strong despite volume miss.

cases.name: Deferred Revenue Change
cases.input: Did deferred revenue increase or decrease vs plan?
cases.expectedOutput: Deferred revenue decreased $2.1M, more than the $0.8M planned decrease, due to longer billing cycles for enterprise customers. Unplanned reduction of $1.3M creates a headwind for Q1 revenue recognition.

cases.name: Sensitivity Analysis
cases.input: How sensitive is EBITDA to a $1M change in revenue?
cases.expectedOutput: $1M revenue increase → EBITDA impact = $1M × Gross Margin % (67.3%) = +$0.67M (no opex variable cost assumed at marginal). With minimal variable S&M, incremental EBITDA margin is approximately 62-68% on subscription revenue. This confirms strong operating leverage for revenue upside.

cases.name: Cost Takeout Opportunities
cases.input: Identify specific cost reduction opportunities to recover the EBITDA miss.
cases.expectedOutput: Identified $2.2M annualized: (1) Cloud optimization $0.7M. (2) Vendor consolidation G&A $0.4M. (3) Conference/events rightsizing $0.3M. (4) Open role hiring freeze for non-critical positions $0.5M. (5) Contractor reduction R&D $0.3M. These are achievable in 2 quarters.

cases.name: Q1 Outlook Adjustments
cases.input: Given Q4 variance, what adjustments are warranted for Q1 FY2026 guidance?
cases.expectedOutput: Q1 guide adjustments: Revenue raise +$3.1M for slipped enterprise deals (high probability close). COGS remain elevated (structural). OpEx: restore planned headcount adds ($0.8M). Net EBITDA Q1 guide: $16.5M-$17.5M vs original $17.8M — reduction reflects deferred G&A investments and continued hosting inflation.

cases.name: Variance by Cost Center
cases.input: Break down opex variance by cost center.
cases.expectedOutput: Cost center variance: Sales -$1.3M favorable (open roles, lower commissions), Marketing -$0.7M favorable (deferred campaigns), Engineering -$0.32M favorable (contractor delays), G&A -$0.5M favorable (legal timing). All cost centers favorable, supporting the narrative that opex management was proactive.

cases.name: Recurring Revenue Variance
cases.input: How did recurring vs non-recurring revenue variance compare?
cases.expectedOutput: Recurring revenue (subscription + support) missed by $3.1M. Non-recurring (PS) missed by $1.1M. Subscription miss is more concerning as it affects ARR run-rate; PS miss is project timing.

cases.name: SMB Segment Performance
cases.input: How did the SMB segment perform vs plan?
cases.expectedOutput: SMB revenue $12.4M vs $12.8M plan (-3%). New logo ARR $2.1M vs $3.2M plan (significant miss). SMB NRR 97% vs 102% target. SMB underperformance is structural (high churn, low ACV) — reassess investment level.

cases.name: Pipeline Coverage vs Plan
cases.input: Is our sales pipeline sufficient to close the Q1 forecast?
cases.expectedOutput: Q1 pipeline coverage is 2.4x vs the 3x target. Coverage has declined from 3.2x in Q3. At historical 33% win rate, current pipeline yields $14.4M booking, short of $18M Q1 target. Requires either win rate improvement or additional pipeline generation in January.

cases.name: Expansion vs New Logo Mix
cases.input: Is the balance between expansion and new logo revenue healthy?
cases.expectedOutput: Expansion was $8M (44% of net new ARR), new logos $14M (78%), partially offset by churn $4M. The increasing reliance on expansion (was 32% last year) is healthy but makes total ARR more sensitive to installed base health.

cases.name: CS Coverage Model
cases.input: Is our CS team appropriately scaled for the ARR base?
cases.expectedOutput: CS ratio: $312M ARR / 42 CSMs = $7.4M ARR per CSM. Benchmark for complex SaaS: $5-8M per CSM. Currently at high end. 2 open roles create $14.8M coverage gap. Risk: renewal coverage slipping in Q1 if not hired by February.

cases.name: Deal Velocity Variance
cases.input: How has sales deal velocity changed and what is the impact?
cases.expectedOutput: Average sales cycle extended from 68 days (Q3) to 84 days (Q4), +24%. Impact: delayed revenue recognition, higher cost per deal. Root causes: procurement delays in enterprise, new legal review requirements for data processing agreements. Mitigation: pre-approved DPA templates in Q1.

cases.name: Bookings Timing Distribution
cases.input: How are bookings distributed within the quarter and what does it imply?
cases.expectedOutput: 82% of Q4 bookings came in the last 2 weeks of December (back-end loaded). This creates risk: any late quarter disruption compounds the miss. Recommendation: accelerate early-quarter closes via incentives, reducing back-loading reliance.

cases.name: Revenue Per Sales Rep
cases.input: What is revenue per sales rep and how does it compare to plan?
cases.expectedOutput: Revenue per quota-carrying rep: $2.1M ARR covered (actual), vs $2.6M at plan quota. Under-attainment was 19% below plan. Quota-carrying headcount on plan at 48. Per-rep productivity is the gap, not headcount.

cases.name: Full Variance Attribution Summary
cases.input: Provide a comprehensive variance attribution for Q4 FY2025 to support the board package.
cases.expectedOutput: Q4 FY2025 vs Budget: Revenue -$4.2M (enterprise deal slippage -$3.1M, PS timing -$1.1M). Gross Margin -80bps (hosting +60bps, CS +40bps, partially offset by mix +20bps). EBITDA -$2.8M (revenue miss -$4.2M, COGS headwinds -$1.4M, opex underspend +$2.8M). FX impact -$0.6M on revenue. One-time charges -$1.3M. Normalized EBITDA was in-line with budget.
